seoTitle: NCE Products | MOSFET, IGBT, Power Module | LiTong
seoDescription: Browse NCE Power products including MOSFETs, IGBTs, power modules, and GaN devices. Technical support available for EV, renewable energy, and industrial applications.

faqs.question: What product categories does NCE offer?
faqs.answer: NCE offers four main product categories: MOSFETs including Trench MOSFETs, Super Junction MOSFETs, and SGTMOS for various power switching applications; IGBTs for motor control and power conversion; Power Modules including intelligent power modules for integrated solutions; and GaN Devices for next-generation high-frequency applications. Each category covers a wide range of voltage and current ratings to meet diverse application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories below or contact our FAE team for selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right NCE product for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right NCE product involves: (1) Identify your application requirements - voltage rating, current capacity, switching frequency, and package type. (2) Review the product category that matches your needs - MOSFETs for high-frequency switching, IGBTs for high-power motor control, Power Modules for integrated solutions, or GaN for highest efficiency. (3) Compare specifications using our parametric tables. (4) Check reference designs and application notes. (5) Contact our FAE team for technical consultation and sample support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for personalized product recommendations based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of NCE's Super Junction MOSFET technology?
faqs.answer: NCE's Super Junction MOSFET technology provides several advantages: (1) Lower Rds(on) - significantly reduced on-resistance compared to conventional planar MOSFETs, resulting in lower conduction losses. (2) Faster switching - reduced gate charge and output capacitance enable higher switching frequencies. (3) Better efficiency - lower switching and conduction losses improve overall system efficiency by 2-5%. (4) Smaller size - lower Rds(on) allows smaller die size for same current rating, reducing cost. (5) Wide voltage range - available from 500V to 900V for various applications. (6) Proven reliability - extensive testing and field validation in demanding applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Super Junction MOSFETs for high-voltage, high-efficiency applications above 500V.

categories.name: MOSFETs
categories.description: NCE MOSFETs include Trench MOSFETs for low-voltage applications, Super Junction MOSFETs for high-voltage high-efficiency switching, and SGTMOS for advanced power management.
categories.longDescription: NCE's MOSFET portfolio leverages advanced trench and super junction technologies to deliver industry-leading performance. The product range includes Trench MOSFETs (30V-200V) for low-voltage high-current applications such as DC-DC converters and motor drives; Super Junction MOSFETs (500V-900V) for high-efficiency power supplies, LED drivers, and solar inverters; and SGTMOS (Shielded Gate Trench MOSFET) for improved EMI performance and reduced switching losses. All NCE MOSFETs feature low Rds(on), fast switching, and excellent reliability, making them ideal for cost-sensitive applications requiring high performance. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive MOSFETs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: Trench MOSFETs
series.description: Low-voltage high-current MOSFETs for DC-DC and motor applications

series.name: Super Junction MOSFETs
series.description: High-voltage MOSFETs with ultra-low Rds(on) for power supplies

selectionGuide.title: How to Select NCE MOSFETs
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting Trench and Super Junction MOSFETs

faqs.question: How do I choose between Trench and Super Junction MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: The choice depends on your voltage requirements: (1) Trench MOSFETs (30V-200V) are ideal for low-voltage high-current applications such as DC-DC converters, motor drives, and battery management systems. They offer very low Rds(on) and fast switching. (2) Super Junction MOSFETs (500V-900V) are designed for high-voltage applications such as AC-DC power supplies, LED drivers, and solar inverters. They provide much lower Rds(on) than conventional planar MOSFETs at high voltages. (3) For applications between 200V-500V, evaluate both technologies based on switching frequency and efficiency requirements. (4) Super Junction devices typically have higher switching speeds but may require more careful gate drive design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Trench MOSFETs for <200V, Super Junction for >500V applications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SGTMOS and standard Trench MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: SGTMOS (Shielded Gate Trench MOSFET) offers several advantages over standard Trench MOSFETs: (1) Lower gate charge (Qg) - the shielded gate structure reduces Miller capacitance, resulting in faster switching and lower gate drive losses. (2) Improved EMI performance - reduced dv/dt during switching reduces electromagnetic interference. (3) Better switching behavior - softer switching transitions reduce voltage overshoot and ringing. (4) Higher efficiency - lower switching losses enable higher switching frequencies or improved efficiency at same frequency. (5) Trade-offs - SGTMOS may have slightly higher Rds(on) than comparable standard Trench devices. (6) Applications - SGTMOS is ideal for high-frequency applications (>100kHz) and noise-sensitive designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SGTMOS for high-frequency or EMI-sensitive applications.

products.name: 200V 60A Trench MOSFET
products.shortDescription: NCE NCEP60T20 200V 60A N-channel Trench MOSFET with ultra-low Rds(on) for high-current motor control and power conver...
descriptionParagraphs: The NCEP60T20 is a 200V 60A N-channel Trench MOSFET designed for high-current motor control and power conversion.
Features ultra-low Rds(on) of 5.5mΩ typical at Vgs=10V, minimizing conduction losses.
Available in TO-220 and TO-263 packages with excellent thermal performance.

faeReview.author: 张伟强
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The NCEP60T20 is one of NCE's most popular low-voltage MOSFETs for motor control applications. I've successfully used this device in numerous DC motor drive and power tool projects. The 5.5mΩ Rds(on) keeps conduction losses low even at 30-40A continuous current. One brushless motor controller project achieved 98% efficiency using this MOSFET with proper gate drive. The 200V rating provides good margin for 48V and 72V battery systems with regenerative braking. For gate drive, I recommend 10-15Ω gate resistors for optimal switching performance. The TO-263 package handles the thermal requirements well with proper PCB copper area. This device offers excellent value compared to international brands with similar specs.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent cost-performance ratio for motor control applications

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current rating with lower Rds(on)
alternativeParts.useCase: Use when higher motor power is needed

faqs.question: What is the recommended gate drive voltage for NCEP60T20?
faqs.answer: The NCEP60T20 is recommended to be driven with +10V to +12V for turn-on and 0V for turn-off. This provides: (1) Full enhancement of the MOSFET for lowest Rds(on). The device is fully enhanced at Vgs=10V with Rds(on) of 5.5mΩ typical. (2) Fast switching transitions to minimize switching losses. (3) Good noise immunity to prevent false triggering. The gate threshold voltage is 2-4V, so 10V provides adequate margin. (4) Gate drive circuit should provide peak currents of at least 2A for fast switching given the 65nC gate charge. (5) For high dv/dt applications, consider using negative turn-off voltage (-3V to -5V) for improved noise immunity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +10V/0V gate drive for standard applications; consult FAE for high noise environments.

faqs.question: What is the typical switching frequency for NCEP60T20?
faqs.answer: The NCEP60T20 is optimized for switching frequencies in the 20-100kHz range. At these frequencies: (1) Switching losses are manageable with standard gate drive. The device has Qg=65nC and Qgd=15nC typical. (2) Motor control applications typically use 20-50kHz to avoid audible noise while maintaining good control response. (3) DC-DC converter applications can use higher frequencies up to 100kHz or more with proper gate drive. (4) For frequencies above 100kHz, careful attention to gate drive strength and PCB layout is needed to minimize switching losses. (5) The device can also be used at lower frequencies (<20kHz) where conduction losses dominate. At 100kHz with 30A load, switching losses are approximately 10-15W.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 20-50kHz for motor control; up to 100kHz for DC-DC converters.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the power dissipation for NCEP60T20?
faqs.answer: Total power dissipation in NCEP60T20 consists of conduction losses and switching losses: (1) Conduction loss = I² × Rds(on) × duty cycle. At 30A with 5.5mΩ Rds(on) and 80% duty cycle: Pcond = 900 × 0.0055 × 0.8 = 3.96W. (2) Switching loss = (Eon + Eoff) × switching frequency. At 50kHz, this is typically 5-8W depending on switching speed. (3) Total loss = conduction loss + switching loss. For thermal design, ensure the junction temperature stays below 175°C. With Rth(j-c) = 0.5°C/W and Rth(c-s) = 0.3°C/W, a heatsink with thermal resistance <3°C/W is recommended for 15W dissipation at 50°C ambient. (4) Use thermal simulation for accurate prediction.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate both conduction and switching losses; use adequate heatsinking.

faqs.question: What protection features should be implemented with NCEP60T20?
faqs.answer: Recommended protection circuits for NCEP60T20 include: (1) Overcurrent protection - use current sense resistor with comparator or controller overcurrent detection. Response time should be <10μs. (2) Overvoltage protection - TVS diodes or zener clamps across drain-source to suppress inductive spikes. Select clamp voltage below 200V rating with margin. (3) Overtemperature protection - NTC thermistor monitoring heatsink or PCB temperature with shutdown at 100-125°C. (4) Undervoltage lockout - prevent operation if gate drive voltage drops below 8V. (5) Shoot-through protection - for half-bridge or full-bridge configurations, implement dead time control to prevent simultaneous conduction. (6) Gate protection - TVS diodes or zener clamps to protect gate from overvoltage (>20V).
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement all six protections for reliable operation in motor control applications.

faqs.question: Can NCEP60T20 be used in parallel for higher current?
faqs.answer: Yes, NCEP60T20 can be paralleled for higher current applications, but careful design is required: (1) Static current sharing - use MOSFETs from the same production lot with matched Rds(on). Variation should be <10% for good sharing. (2) Dynamic current sharing - symmetric layout with equal drain and source inductance is critical. Use Kelvin source connections for gate drive. (3) Individual gate resistors - use separate gate resistor (5-10Ω) for each MOSFET to prevent oscillation. (4) Layout - parallel connection at the drain and source terminals, not through PCB traces. (5) Derating - parallel 3 devices for 2x current capability to ensure reliability margin. (6) Testing - verify current sharing with current probes under full load. (7) Thermal design - ensure equal thermal resistance to heatsink for each device. With proper design, paralleled NCEP60T20s can handle 100A+ continuous current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE for parallel operation design review and layout recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the difference between NCEP60T20 and NCEP80T20?
faqs.answer: The main differences between NCEP60T20 and NCEP80T20 are: (1) Current rating - NCEP80T20 is rated for 80A continuous current vs 60A for NCEP60T20 (+33% higher). (2) Rds(on) - NCEP80T20 has 4.2mΩ typical Rds(on) vs 5.5mΩ for NCEP60T20, resulting in lower conduction losses. (3) Die size - NCEP80T20 uses a larger die for higher current capability. (4) Gate charge - NCEP80T20 has slightly higher Qg (80nC vs 65nC) due to larger die size. (5) Package - both available in TO-220 and TO-263 packages. (6) Price - NCEP80T20 is approximately 20-25% higher cost. (7) Applications - NCEP60T20 for 20-40A applications, NCEP80T20 for 40-60A applications. Both have the same 200V voltage rating and similar switching characteristics, making them pin-compatible alternatives.

products.name: 650V 20A Super Junction MOSFET
products.shortDescription: NCE NCE65T540 650V 20A N-channel Super Junction MOSFET with ultra-low Rds(on) for high-efficiency power supplies and ...
descriptionParagraphs: The NCE65T540 is a 650V 20A N-channel Super Junction MOSFET designed for high-efficiency power conversion.
Features ultra-low Rds(on) of 0.19Ω typical at Vgs=10V using advanced super junction technology.
Excellent switching characteristics with low gate charge for high-frequency applications.

faeReview.author: 李伟明
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Supply Applications
faeReview.content: The NCE65T540 is an excellent Super Junction MOSFET for high-voltage power supply applications. I've used this device in numerous 500W+ power supply designs with excellent results. The 0.19Ω Rds(on) is very competitive for a 650V device, resulting in low conduction losses. One 48V/10A power supply project achieved 94% efficiency at full load using this MOSFET. The low gate charge (28nC) enables switching at 100kHz with reasonable gate drive losses. The TO-220F package provides good thermal performance with lower profile than standard TO-220. For gate drive, I recommend 10Ω gate resistors for 100kHz operation. The device has proven very reliable - we've had excellent results in solar inverter applications. For new designs, this device offers great value compared to international brands.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent efficiency and value for high-voltage power supplies

faqs.question: What is the advantage of Super Junction technology in NCE65T540?
faqs.answer: Super Junction technology provides significant advantages over conventional planar MOSFETs: (1) Much lower Rds(on) - the super junction structure reduces on-resistance by 3-5x compared to planar devices at same voltage rating. This results in lower conduction losses. (2) Better switching performance - lower gate charge and output capacitance enable faster switching and lower switching losses. (3) Higher efficiency - combined lower conduction and switching losses improve overall system efficiency by 2-5%. (4) Smaller die size - for same Rds(on), super junction devices use smaller die, reducing cost. (5) Cooler operation - lower losses mean less heat generation, simplifying thermal design. (6) Higher power density - enables smaller, lighter power supplies. The trade-off is slightly more complex gate drive requirements due to fast switching.

faqs.question: How does NCE65T540 compare to CoolMOS devices?
faqs.answer: NCE65T540 offers competitive performance compared to CoolMOS and other super junction devices: (1) Rds(on) - at 0.19Ω, it matches or exceeds many CoolMOS C6 series devices. (2) Gate charge - Qg of 28nC is comparable to similar rated devices. (3) Switching speed - similar dv/dt and di/dt characteristics. (4) Price - typically 20-30% lower cost than equivalent CoolMOS devices. (5) Availability - reliable supply from NCE's manufacturing facilities. (6) Quality - AEC-Q101 qualified for automotive applications. (7) Support - local FAE support in Chinese and English. The main difference is that CoolMOS has longer track record in market, while NCE offers better value. For most applications, NCE65T540 provides equivalent performance at lower cost.

faqs.question: What is the recommended snubber circuit for NCE65T540?
faqs.answer: For NCE65T540 in hard-switching applications, recommended snubber circuits: (1) RC snubber - 10-47Ω resistor in series with 100pF-1nF capacitor across drain-source. Values depend on switching frequency and stray inductance. (2) RCD snubber - for high-power applications, use resistor-capacitor-diode network to clamp voltage spikes. (3) No snubber - may be acceptable for low-inductance layouts with good PCB design. (4) Soft switching - consider resonant topologies to eliminate need for snubbers. For typical power supply applications with <100nH stray inductance, a 22Ω + 470pF RC snubber is usually sufficient. Always verify with oscilloscope measurements during prototype testing. The fast switching of super junction devices can cause voltage overshoot - proper snubber design is essential.

faqs.question: Can NCE65T540 be used in LLC resonant converters?
faqs.answer: Yes, NCE65T540 is well-suited for LLC resonant converter applications: (1) Zero voltage switching (ZVS) - the device achieves ZVS at turn-on, eliminating turn-on losses. (2) Low output capacitance - Coss of 65pF typical enables fast transition and good ZVS performance. (3) Body diode - the integrated body diode can be used for freewheeling, though external diode may be preferred for highest efficiency. (4) Gate drive - use 10-15Ω gate resistors for optimal switching. (5) Frequency range - suitable for 100-500kHz LLC operation. (6) Efficiency - super junction devices achieve excellent efficiency in LLC topology, often 96-98%. (7) Thermal design - even with high efficiency, provide adequate heatsinking for 10-20W dissipation. The NCE65T540's low Rds(on) and fast switching make it an excellent choice for high-efficiency LLC designs.

faqs.question: What PCB layout considerations are important for NCE65T540?
faqs.answer: Critical PCB layout considerations for NCE65T540: (1) Minimize stray inductance - keep high-current loops as small as possible. Use wide, short traces for drain and source connections. (2) Kelvin source connection - separate power source from gate drive source connection to prevent feedback. (3) Gate drive loop - minimize loop area between gate driver output, gate resistor, and MOSFET gate. (4) Decoupling capacitors - place 100nF ceramic capacitor close to gate driver supply pins. Add bulk capacitor for high-frequency filtering. (5) Thermal vias - use multiple thermal vias under the MOSFET tab for heat dissipation. (6) Clearance - maintain adequate creepage and clearance distances for 650V operation. (7) Current sensing - place sense resistor in low-side source path for clean signals. (8) Snubber placement - place snubber components close to drain and source terminals.

faqs.question: How do I select the gate resistor for NCE65T540?
faqs.answer: Gate resistor selection for NCE65T540: (1) Switching speed vs EMI trade-off - lower resistance gives faster switching (lower losses) but higher EMI and voltage overshoot. Higher resistance slows switching (higher losses) but reduces EMI. (2) Typical values - 5-20Ω for most applications. Start with 10Ω and adjust based on testing. (3) Power dissipation - gate resistor power = Qg × Vgs × fsw. At 100kHz: 28nC × 10V × 100kHz = 28mW. Use 1/4W or larger resistor. (4) dv/dt control - gate resistor controls turn-on dv/dt. For NCE65T540, target 5-10V/ns. (5) Separate resistors - use separate resistors for turn-on and turn-off if different speeds needed. (6) Testing - measure gate waveform and switching losses with oscilloscope. Adjust resistor value for optimal balance of losses and EMI. (7) Temperature - consider resistor temperature coefficient for stable operation.

categories.name: IGBTs
categories.description: NCE IGBTs include discrete IGBTs and intelligent power modules for motor control, inverter, and power conversion applications.
categories.longDescription: NCE's IGBT portfolio provides high-performance solutions for motor control and power conversion applications. The product range includes discrete IGBTs (600V-1700V) for industrial drives, home appliances, and new energy vehicle applications; and intelligent power modules (IPMs) that integrate IGBTs with gate drivers and protection circuits for simplified design. NCE IGBTs feature low Vce(sat), fast switching, and high ruggedness, making them ideal for demanding applications requiring reliable performance. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive IGBTs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: Discrete IGBTs
series.description: High-power IGBTs for motor drives and inverters

series.name: IPMs
series.description: Intelligent power modules with integrated drivers

selectionGuide.title: How to Select NCE IGBTs
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting IGBTs and IPMs

faqs.question: How do I choose between discrete IGBTs and IPMs?
faqs.answer: IPMs (Intelligent Power Modules) integrate IGBT chips with gate drive circuits, protection functions, and sometimes bootstrap circuits in a single package. Advantages of IPMs include simplified design, faster time-to-market, higher reliability with integrated protection, and compact size. Discrete IGBTs offer more flexibility in gate driver selection and are preferred for high-power applications requiring custom drive solutions. Choose IPMs for faster development and discrete IGBTs for high-power custom designs.

faqs.question: What is the difference between IGBT and MOSFET?
faqs.answer: IGBTs are preferred for 600V and above with higher current capacity, while MOSFETs are better for lower voltages. IGBTs typically offer better conduction characteristics at higher currents and lower cost per amp at high voltages. MOSFETs have lower switching losses and are preferred for high-frequency applications (>20kHz). Choose IGBTs for motor drives and inverters above 600V, MOSFETs for switching power supplies and DC-DC converters below 200V.

products.name: 600V 20A IGBT
products.shortDescription: NCE NCE20N60 600V 20A IGBT with low Vce(sat) for motor control and inverter applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The NCE20N60 is a 600V 20A IGBT designed for motor control and power conversion.
Features low Vce(sat) of 1.7V typical, minimizing conduction losses.
Available in TO-220 and TO-247 packages with excellent thermal performance.

faeReview.author: 王建华
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Motor Control
faeReview.content: The NCE20N60 is a reliable 600V IGBT for motor control applications. I've used this device in numerous inverter projects with good results. The 1.7V Vce(sat) keeps conduction losses reasonable for a 600V device. One 2kW motor drive project achieved 96% efficiency using this IGBT. The switching characteristics are well-suited for 10-20kHz PWM frequencies typical in motor control. For thermal design, the TO-247 package handles about 80W dissipation with proper heatsinking. This device offers good value for cost-sensitive motor control applications.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable performance for motor control applications

faqs.question: What is the recommended gate drive voltage for NCE20N60?
faqs.answer: The NCE20N60 is recommended to be driven with +15V for turn-on and 0V for turn-off. This provides full enhancement of the IGBT for lowest on-state voltage drop and fast switching transitions.

faqs.question: What is the typical switching frequency for NCE20N60?
faqs.answer: The NCE20N60 is optimized for switching frequencies in the 5-20kHz range, which is typical for motor control applications.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the power dissipation for NCE20N60?
faqs.answer: Total power dissipation consists of conduction losses (Ic × Vce(sat) × duty cycle) and switching losses ((Eon + Eoff) × switching frequency).

faqs.question: What protection features should be implemented?
faqs.answer: Recommended protections include overcurrent protection, short circuit protection, overtemperature protection, and undervoltage lockout.

faqs.question: Can NCE20N60 be used in parallel?
faqs.answer: Yes, but careful design is required for current sharing including matched devices, symmetric layout, and individual gate resistors.

faqs.question: What is the difference between NCE20N60 and NCE30N60?
faqs.answer: NCE30N60 has higher current rating (30A vs 20A) and uses larger die, but similar Vce(sat) characteristics.

products.name: 1200V 15A IGBT
products.shortDescription: High-quality igbts component designed for reliable performance in industrial and commercial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The NCE15N120 is a 1200V 15A IGBT designed for high-voltage applications.
Features low Vce(sat) and fast switching for high-efficiency designs.
Suitable for 380V AC applications and solar inverters.

faeReview.author: 王建华
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Motor Control
faeReview.content: The NCE15N120 is ideal for 380V AC applications. Good performance in solar inverter designs.
faeReview.highlight: High voltage rating for industrial applications

faqs.question: What applications are suitable for NCE15N120?
faqs.answer: NCE15N120 is suitable for 380V AC motor drives, solar inverters, UPS systems, and induction heating applications requiring 1200V rating.

faqs.question: What is the maximum junction temperature?
faqs.answer: Maximum junction temperature is 150°C. Design for Tj ≤ 125°C for reliable operation.

faqs.question: How does NCE15N120 compare to 600V IGBTs?
faqs.answer: NCE15N120 has higher voltage rating (1200V vs 600V) suitable for 380V AC applications, with slightly higher Vce(sat).

faqs.question: What gate drive is recommended?
faqs.answer: Use +15V/0V gate drive with 10-20Ω gate resistors for optimal switching performance.

faqs.question: Can this device be used for soft switching?
faqs.answer: Yes, NCE15N120 can be used in soft-switching resonant converters with appropriate design.

faqs.question: What is the switching frequency range?
faqs.answer: Optimized for 5-20kHz switching frequency typical for motor control and inverter applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 5-20kHz for optimal balance of efficiency and switching losses.

categories.name: Power Modules
categories.description: NCE Power Modules include intelligent power modules (IPMs) and custom power solutions for industrial and automotive applications.
categories.longDescription: NCE's Power Module portfolio provides integrated solutions combining multiple power devices with gate drivers and protection circuits. The product range includes Intelligent Power Modules (IPMs) for motor control applications, PIMs (Power Integrated Modules) for inverters, and custom modules for specific customer requirements. These modules simplify design, reduce time-to-market, and improve system reliability by integrating proven power stage designs with comprehensive protection features. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Power Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: IPMs
series.description: Intelligent Power Modules with integrated gate drivers

selectionGuide.title: How to Select NCE Power Modules
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting IPMs and PIMs

faqs.question: What are the advantages of using IPMs vs discrete solutions?
faqs.answer: IPMs offer several advantages: (1) Simplified design - integrated gate drivers, protection circuits, and bootstrap diodes reduce external component count. (2) Faster time-to-market - pre-designed and tested power stage reduces development time. (3) Higher reliability - factory-tested modules with optimized layout and thermal design. (4) Compact size - integrated solution reduces PCB area. (5) Built-in protection - overcurrent, short circuit, undervoltage, and overtemperature protection included. (6) Better performance - optimized switching characteristics and lower EMI. The main trade-off is less flexibility in gate drive design and higher cost for low-volume applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use IPMs for faster development and higher reliability; discrete for cost-sensitive high-volume applications.

faqs.question: How do I select the right IPM for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right IPM involves: (1) Voltage rating - choose based on DC bus voltage with 1.5-2x safety margin. (2) Current rating - select based on motor RMS current with 1.5x overload margin. (3) Topology - choose 6-pack for three-phase motors, half-bridge for single-phase or DC motors. (4) Control interface - verify compatibility with your MCU (HIN/LIN, PWM, etc.). (5) Protection features - ensure required protections are included. (6) Thermal design - check thermal resistance and required heatsink. (7) Package - consider mounting and space constraints. NCE provides selection guides and reference designs for common applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE for IPM selection assistance and reference designs.

products.name: 600V 20A Intelligent Power Module
products.shortDescription: NCE NCEIPM20 600V 20A three-phase IPM with integrated gate drivers and protection for motor control applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The NCEIPM20 is a 600V 20A three-phase intelligent power module designed for motor control.
Integrates six IGBTs with gate drivers, bootstrap diodes, and comprehensive protection circuits.
Simplifies motor drive design with pre-optimized power stage and built-in protections.
features: Integrated gate drivers with bootstrap diodes

faeReview.author: 赵志强
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Motor Control
faeReview.content: The NCEIPM20 is an excellent choice for small to medium power motor drives. I've used this IPM in numerous air conditioner and washing machine inverter projects. The integrated design saves significant development time - what would take weeks with discrete components can be done in days. The built-in protections work reliably - overcurrent detection responds in microseconds to protect the IGBTs. One 1.5kW air conditioner compressor drive project used this IPM with excellent results. The DIP package is easy to assemble and provides good thermal performance with proper heatsinking. For control, the HIN/LIN interface is compatible with most MCUs. Overall, a very reliable and cost-effective solution for motor control up to 1.5kW.
faeReview.highlight: Integrated solution saves development time and improves reliability

faqs.question: What control interface does NCEIPM20 use?
faqs.answer: NCEIPM20 uses HIN/LIN (High-side Input / Low-side Input) control interface. Each of the six IGBTs has independent high-side and low-side control inputs. The control signals are typically 3.3V or 5V logic level compatible. Dead time control must be implemented in the external controller to prevent shoot-through. The module includes internal bootstrap circuits for high-side gate drive, requiring only a single low-voltage supply.

faqs.question: What protection features are built into NCEIPM20?
faqs.answer: NCEIPM20 includes comprehensive protection features: (1) Overcurrent protection - detects overcurrent through external shunt resistor and shuts down all IGBTs. (2) Undervoltage lockout - disables operation if supply voltage drops below threshold. (3) Overtemperature protection - internal temperature sensor shuts down at 125°C. (4) Shoot-through prevention - internal interlock prevents simultaneous high-side and low-side conduction. (5) Fault output - open-drain fault signal indicates protection activation.

faqs.question: How do I design the heatsink for NCEIPM20?
faqs.answer: Thermal design for NCEIPM20: (1) Calculate losses - at 20A with 1.7V Vce(sat) and 10kHz switching, total losses are approximately 40-50W. (2) Thermal resistance - module Rth(j-c) is typically 1.5°C/W. (3) Heatsink calculation - for Tj max 125°C and Ta 50°C, required Rth(s-a) = (125-50)/50 - 1.5 = 0°C/W. This requires forced air cooling or large heatsink. (4) Thermal interface - use thermal grease or pad between module and heatsink. (5) Mounting - ensure even pressure distribution across module base.

faqs.question: Can NCEIPM20 be used for servo motor control?
faqs.answer: Yes, NCEIPM20 is suitable for servo motor control applications: (1) Current rating - 20A continuous suits most servo motors up to 1.5kW. (2) Switching speed - suitable for 10-20kHz PWM typical in servo drives. (3) Protection - built-in protections ensure reliable operation in dynamic servo applications. (4) Control - HIN/LIN interface compatible with servo control algorithms. (5) Feedback - external current sensing required for servo control loops. The module's reliability and integrated protections make it well-suited for industrial servo applications.

faqs.question: What is the bootstrap circuit in NCEIPM20?
faqs.answer: NCEIPM20 includes internal bootstrap circuits for high-side gate drive: (1) Bootstrap diode - internal fast recovery diode charges bootstrap capacitor. (2) Bootstrap capacitor - external capacitor (typically 10-47μF) provides high-side gate drive voltage. (3) Operation - low-side IGBT turn-on charges capacitor; capacitor powers high-side driver when low-side is off. (4) Voltage - bootstrap voltage is typically 15V above VS pin (high-side emitter). (5) Refresh - capacitor must be refreshed by periodic low-side turn-on. Minimum refresh frequency depends on capacitor size and leakage.

faqs.question: How does NCEIPM20 compare to discrete solutions?
faqs.answer: NCEIPM20 vs discrete IGBT solution comparison: (1) Development time - IPM reduces development from weeks to days. (2) Component count - IPM integrates 6 IGBTs, 6 gate drivers, 6 bootstrap diodes, and protection circuits vs 20+ discrete components. (3) PCB area - IPM requires significantly less board space. (4) Reliability - factory-tested and optimized design vs discrete design variability. (5) Cost - IPM higher component cost but lower total system cost when including reduced design time and higher reliability. (6) Flexibility - discrete offers more customization; IPM provides proven optimized design.

products.name: 1200V 50A PIM Module
products.shortDescription: NCE NCEPIM50 1200V 50A Power Integrated Module for three-phase inverters and motor drives.
descriptionParagraphs: The NCEPIM50 is a 1200V 50A Power Integrated Module for high-power inverter applications.
Integrates three-phase rectifier, brake unit, and three-phase inverter in one package.
Ideal for solar inverters, motor drives, and UPS systems up to 30kW.

faeReview.author: 赵志强
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Motor Control
faeReview.content: The NCEPIM50 is a comprehensive power solution for high-power inverters. The integrated rectifier, brake, and inverter in one package simplifies design significantly. I've used this in 15kW solar inverter projects with excellent efficiency (98%+). The 1200V rating provides good margin for 380V AC applications. Thermal design requires careful attention - water cooling recommended for continuous 50A operation. The low inductance design reduces switching losses and EMI. Overall, an excellent choice for high-power applications requiring integrated solutions.
faeReview.highlight: Comprehensive integrated solution for high-power inverters

faqs.question: What is included in NCEPIM50?
faqs.answer: NCEPIM50 includes: (1) Three-phase rectifier - six diodes for AC-DC conversion. (2) Three-phase inverter - six IGBTs with freewheeling diodes for DC-AC conversion. (3) Brake chopper - IGBT and diode for dynamic braking. (4) NTC sensor - temperature monitoring for thermal protection. (5) All components integrated in single package with optimized layout. This comprehensive integration simplifies inverter design significantly.

faqs.question: What cooling is required for NCEPIM50?
faqs.answer: NCEPIM50 thermal management: (1) Heat dissipation - at 50A with 2V drop, losses are approximately 300W per phase. (2) Forced air cooling - sufficient for light load or intermittent operation. (3) Water cooling - recommended for continuous 50A operation. (4) Thermal interface - use high-performance thermal grease between module and heatsink. (5) Mounting - ensure uniform pressure across module base for good thermal contact. (6) Temperature monitoring - use built-in NTC for overtemperature protection.

faqs.question: Can NCEPIM50 be used for solar inverters?
faqs.answer: Yes, NCEPIM50 is well-suited for solar inverter applications: (1) Voltage rating - 1200V handles 1000V DC bus typical in commercial solar inverters. (2) Current rating - 50A supports 15-20kW inverter designs. (3) Integrated rectifier - useful for battery charging in hybrid inverters. (4) Brake chopper - handles regenerative energy during grid faults. (5) Efficiency - optimized layout achieves 98%+ efficiency. (6) Reliability - industrial-grade construction for 20+ year life. The module is used in numerous commercial solar installations.

faqs.question: What gate drive is required for NCEPIM50?
faqs.answer: NCEPIM50 gate drive requirements: (1) External gate drivers required - module does not include integrated drivers. (2) Isolated drivers - use isolated gate drivers for high-side IGBTs. (3) Drive voltage - +15V typical for turn-on, 0V or -5V for turn-off. (4) Gate resistors - 5-10Ω typical, adjust based on switching speed requirements. (5) Dead time - implement 2-3μs dead time in controller to prevent shoot-through. (6) Protection - external overcurrent and desaturation detection recommended.

faqs.question: What is the switching frequency range?
faqs.answer: NCEPIM50 switching frequency: (1) Optimal range - 5-15kHz for best efficiency and thermal performance. (2) Maximum frequency - up to 20kHz possible with derating. (3) Solar inverters - typically use 8-16kHz to minimize filter size while maintaining efficiency. (4) Motor drives - 5-10kHz typical to avoid audible noise. (5) Trade-offs - higher frequency reduces filter size but increases switching losses. Lower frequency improves efficiency but requires larger filters.

faqs.question: How does NCEPIM50 compare to discrete modules?
faqs.answer: NCEPIM50 vs discrete module comparison: (1) Integration - PIM integrates rectifier, brake, and inverter vs separate modules. (2) Layout - optimized internal layout reduces stray inductance vs discrete connections. (3) Reliability - single package eliminates interconnect failure points. (4) Assembly - one component to mount vs multiple modules. (5) Cost - PIM typically lower total system cost. (6) Flexibility - discrete offers more configuration options; PIM provides proven optimized solution. For most inverter applications, PIM offers the best balance of performance, reliability, and cost.

categories.name: GaN Devices
categories.description: NCE GaN (Gallium Nitride) power devices offer ultra-high efficiency and switching speed for next-generation power electronics.
categories.longDescription: NCE's GaN power device portfolio leverages advanced Gallium Nitride technology to deliver breakthrough performance in power conversion. GaN devices offer significantly lower switching losses, higher switching frequencies, and improved efficiency compared to silicon MOSFETs and IGBTs. These devices are ideal for high-frequency power supplies, data center power, EV onboard chargers, and renewable energy applications where efficiency and power density are critical. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive GaN Devices selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: Enhancement-mode GaN
series.description: Normally-off GaN HEMTs for high-efficiency applications

series.name: Integrated GaN
series.description: GaN with integrated driver for simplified design

selectionGuide.title: How to Select NCE GaN Devices
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting GaN power devices

faqs.question: What are the advantages of GaN vs silicon MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: GaN devices offer significant advantages: (1) Lower switching losses - 10x lower Qg and faster switching enable MHz operation. (2) Higher efficiency - combined lower switching and conduction losses improve efficiency by 1-3%. (3) Higher frequency - can operate at 500kHz-1MHz vs 100kHz for silicon. (4) Smaller magnetics - higher frequency enables smaller transformers and inductors. (5) Higher power density - smaller overall solution size. (6) Better thermal performance - lower losses reduce heat generation. Trade-offs include higher cost, more sensitive gate drive requirements, and limited high-voltage options (>650V).
faqs.decisionGuide: Use GaN for high-frequency (>200kHz) or high-efficiency critical applications.

faqs.question: What are the gate drive requirements for GaN devices?
faqs.answer: GaN gate drive requirements differ from silicon MOSFETs: (1) Gate voltage - typically 5V for on-state (not 10-12V like silicon). Higher voltage can damage device. (2) Gate threshold - 1-2V typical, much lower than silicon. (3) Gate drive loop - minimize inductance (<1nH) to prevent oscillation. (4) dv/dt immunity - GaN devices are sensitive to high dv/dt; proper layout essential. (5) Negative turn-off - use -3V to ensure off-state and prevent false turn-on. (6) Isolation - use isolated gate drivers with tight propagation delay matching for half-bridge. (7) PCB layout - critical for stable operation; follow manufacturer guidelines closely.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use dedicated GaN gate drivers; follow layout guidelines carefully.

products.name: 650V 100mΩ GaN HEMT
products.shortDescription: NCE NCE650G10 650V 100mΩ enhancement-mode GaN HEMT for high-frequency power conversion.
descriptionParagraphs: The NCE650G10 is a 650V 100mΩ enhancement-mode GaN HEMT for next-generation power electronics.
Features ultra-low gate charge (5nC) and fast switching for MHz operation.
Ideal for high-efficiency power supplies, EV charging, and data center applications.

faeReview.author: 孙伟
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Advanced Power
faeReview.content: The NCE650G10 is an impressive GaN device that delivers on the promise of GaN technology. I've designed several 500kHz LLC converters with this device achieving 98%+ efficiency. The 5nC gate charge is incredibly low - gate drive losses are negligible even at 1MHz. One 3kW server power supply project achieved 99% efficiency at 50% load using these GaN devices. The DFN package requires careful PCB layout but provides excellent thermal performance. Gate drive is critical - use dedicated GaN drivers with tight loop inductance. The device is sensitive to layout; follow NCE's guidelines exactly. For high-frequency designs, this GaN device offers unbeatable performance at competitive pricing.
faeReview.highlight: Exceptional efficiency and switching performance for high-frequency designs

alternativeParts.reason: Lower Qg for highest frequency applications
Industry standard GaN device for comparison

faqs.question: What is the maximum switching frequency for NCE650G10?
faqs.answer: NCE650G10 can operate at very high frequencies: (1) Practical range - 500kHz to 2MHz for most power supply applications. (2) Maximum frequency - device capable of switching at 5MHz+ but practical limits set by magnetics and gate drive. (3) Efficiency peak - typically 500kHz-1MHz provides best balance of efficiency and component size. (4) Gate drive - at 2MHz, gate drive losses become significant even with low Qg. (5) Magnetics - core losses limit practical frequency; use low-loss ferrite or advanced materials. (6) Layout - critical above 1MHz; minimize all loop inductances. For most designs, 500kHz-1MHz provides optimal performance.

faqs.question: How do I design the PCB layout for NCE650G10?
faqs.answer: PCB layout is critical for GaN devices: (1) Gate loop - minimize inductance to <1nH. Keep gate driver close to device with wide, short traces. (2) Power loop - minimize drain-source loop area to reduce stray inductance and EMI. (3) Kelvin source - use separate source connection for gate return vs power current. (4) Decoupling - place 100nF ceramic capacitor directly adjacent to device terminals. (5) Grounding - use ground planes with minimal via inductance. (6) Clearance - maintain adequate spacing for 650V operation. (7) Thermal - use thermal vias under device for heat dissipation. Follow NCE's layout guidelines exactly for best performance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between GaN and SiC devices?
faqs.answer: GaN vs SiC comparison: (1) Switching speed - GaN has lower capacitance and faster switching; better for high-frequency (>200kHz). SiC has higher voltage capability and better high-temperature performance. (2) Voltage range - GaN typically 650V max; SiC available to 1700V+. (3) Rds(on) - GaN has lower Rds(on) × area product; better for <650V. SiC better for >900V. (4) Cost - GaN generally lower cost for <650V; SiC better value for high voltage. (5) Applications - GaN for server power, telecom; SiC for EV, solar, industrial. (6) Gate drive - both require careful design but different requirements. Choose GaN for high-frequency low-voltage; SiC for high-voltage high-temperature.

faqs.question: Can NCE650G10 be used in bridgeless PFC circuits?
faqs.answer: Yes, NCE650G10 is excellent for bridgeless PFC applications: (1) Fast switching - enables continuous conduction mode (CCM) PFC at high frequency. (2) Low reverse recovery - zero Qrr eliminates diode losses in totem-pole PFC. (3) Efficiency - can achieve 99%+ efficiency in bridgeless PFC designs. (4) Frequency - operate at 100-300kHz to reduce inductor size. (5) Control - compatible with standard PFC controllers. (6) Layout - critical for noise immunity in bridgeless topology; follow guidelines carefully. The device is used in numerous high-efficiency server power supplies with bridgeless PFC front ends.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for NCE650G10?
faqs.answer: NCE650G10 thermal design: (1) Power dissipation - at 10A with 100mΩ Rds(on), conduction loss is 10W; switching losses minimal at moderate frequencies. (2) Thermal resistance - Rth(j-c) approximately 2°C/W for DFN package. (3) Heatsinking - PCB copper area can provide sufficient cooling for <15W dissipation. (4) Thermal vias - use multiple vias under device to spread heat to inner layers. (5) Forced air - may be needed for continuous high-current operation. (6) Temperature - maximum Tj is 150°C; design for <125°C for reliability. The compact DFN package requires good PCB thermal design but eliminates need for external heatsink in many applications.

faqs.question: How does NCE650G10 compare to silicon super junction MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: NCE650G10 vs super junction MOSFET comparison: (1) Switching losses - GaN has 5-10x lower switching losses due to no Qrr and lower capacitance. (2) Frequency - GaN can operate at 5-10x higher frequency. (3) Efficiency - GaN achieves 1-3% higher efficiency, especially at light load. (4) Cost - GaN typically 2-3x higher device cost but may reduce total system cost with smaller magnetics. (5) Drive - GaN requires more careful gate drive design. (6) Voltage - both limited to 650-700V range. For high-frequency designs (>200kHz) or efficiency-critical applications, GaN is superior. For cost-sensitive low-frequency designs, super junction may be preferred.

products.name: 650V 300mΩ Integrated GaN Power IC
products.shortDescription: NCE NCE650G10 650V 300mΩ GaN power IC with integrated gate driver for simplified high-frequency designs.
descriptionParagraphs: The NCE650G30 is a 650V 300mΩ GaN power IC with integrated gate driver and protection.
Simplifies GaN designs by integrating gate drive and protection functions.
Ideal for high-density adapters, chargers, and LED drivers.

faeReview.author: 孙伟
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Advanced Power
faeReview.content: The NCE650G30 with integrated driver makes GaN design much more accessible. The integrated gate drive eliminates the most challenging aspect of GaN design. I've used this in 65W USB-PD charger designs achieving 94% efficiency at full load with power density of 30W/in³. The 5V logic input is compatible with standard PWM controllers. Protection features work well - UVLO prevents damage from low supply. Thermal performance is good for the compact QFN package. For designers new to GaN, this integrated device is the easiest way to get started with GaN technology. Highly recommended for adapter and charger applications.
faeReview.highlight: Integrated driver simplifies GaN design for adapters and chargers

faqs.question: What is the advantage of integrated gate driver?
faqs.answer: Integrated gate driver provides: (1) Simplified design - no need for external gate driver circuit and careful loop layout. (2) Optimized drive - internal driver matched to GaN device characteristics. (3) Protection - integrated UVLO and overcurrent protection. (4) 5V input - compatible with standard logic and PWM controllers. (5) Smaller solution size - single IC vs discrete GaN + driver. (6) Reliability - factory-optimized drive eliminates design variability. The integrated approach makes GaN technology accessible to designers without extensive GaN experience.

faqs.question: What input does NCE650G30 require?
faqs.answer: NCE650G30 control interface: (1) Power supply - 5V or 12V supply for internal gate driver. (2) PWM input - 5V logic level PWM signal. (3) Enable - active high enable input for power sequencing. (4) Fault output - open-drain fault indication. (5) Ground - single ground reference. The simple interface is compatible with standard PWM controllers and microcontrollers. No external gate resistors or complex drive circuits needed.

faqs.question: What protection features are included?
faqs.answer: NCE650G30 protection features: (1) UVLO - disables device if supply voltage drops below threshold. (2) Overcurrent - cycle-by-cycle current limiting protects against overload. (3) Overtemperature - internal thermal shutdown at 150°C. (4) ESD - integrated ESD protection on all pins. (5) dv/dt immunity - internal design provides robustness against dv/dt induced turn-on. These protections ensure reliable operation and simplify system-level protection design.

faqs.question: Can NCE650G30 be used in flyback converters?
faqs.answer: Yes, NCE650G30 is well-suited for flyback applications: (1) Voltage rating - 650V provides margin for 400V DC bus flyback designs. (2) Rds(on) - 300mΩ suitable for <100W flyback converters. (3) Switching speed - fast switching reduces switching losses at high frequency. (4) Frequency - can operate at 200-500kHz to reduce transformer size. (5) Control - compatible with standard flyback PWM controllers. (6) Efficiency - GaN enables >93% efficiency in high-density adapters. The integrated driver simplifies the design significantly compared to discrete GaN solutions.

faqs.question: What is the maximum power for adapter applications?
faqs.answer: NCE650G30 power capability: (1) Thermal limited - approximately 10-15W dissipation capability with PCB cooling. (2) Current limited - 15A pulse rating supports up to 100W adapters. (3) Practical limit - 65-100W for high-density adapters with adequate cooling. (4) Efficiency - at 65W, can achieve 94%+ efficiency in flyback or active clamp flyback. (5) For higher power - consider discrete GaN devices or parallel operation. The device is ideal for USB-PD chargers up to 65W and high-density adapters up to 100W.

faqs.question: How does NCE650G30 compare to discrete GaN solutions?
faqs.answer: NCE650G30 vs discrete GaN comparison: (1) Ease of use - integrated driver eliminates complex gate drive design. (2) Solution size - single IC vs multiple components. (3) Time to market - much faster development with integrated solution. (4) Performance - slightly higher Rds(on) than best discrete devices but still excellent. (5) Cost - may be higher device cost but lower total BOM cost. (6) Flexibility - less flexibility than discrete but optimized for common applications. For designers new to GaN or time-constrained projects, the integrated solution is highly recommended.
